summary: Scorching Help/Slip/Franklin's start, tight Let It Grow, solid second set
blurb: The concert launches with a blazing Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence before moving into Let It Grow and Foolish Heart. A relaxed second set builds through The Other One to a heartfelt Brokedown Palace finale, highlighted by Brent Mydland's organ work.
review: June 24, 1990 at Autzen Stadium captured the Dead in top form despite a rough vocal start on "Help On The Way." Jerry Garcia delivered crisp guitar tones and an inspired midi solo on "Women Are Smarter," while Phil Lesh’s warm bass and occasional ’73‑style licks added depth throughout. Bob Weir’s backing vocals were solid and his New Orleans‑flavored second line on the encore gave the show a unique flavor. Brent Mydland, in what would be one of his final West Coast appearances, supplied energetic organ passages that lifted songs like "Brokedown Palace" and kept the band’s momentum high. The setlist flowed from the high‑energy opening trio into a solid first half that some felt dragged before revving up with "Let It Grow," which many reviewers called an inspired jam. The second set relaxed into a river‑like flow, highlighted by "Loose Lucy," a superb rendition of "The Other One," and a soulful encore. Recording quality varies—soundboard SBD #gd90-06-24.sbd.unknown.12092.sbeok.shnf offers the clearest picture with minor hiss, while the excellent matrix mix (gd1990-06-24.mtx.hansokolow.108329.flac16) provides crystal‑clear audio for those who value fidelity.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive, praising the opening Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence, Let It Grow jam, Brent Mydland's organ work and overall high energy. Two recordings are mixed but still note strong moments, with only minor criticism of early vocal issues. This consistent positivity across multiple sources justifies a moderate‑high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with good clarity, minimal hiss and edits; captures the scorching Help>Slip>Franklin's opening and inspired jams like Let It Grow and Brokedown Palace.

key_highlights: Scorching Help > Slip > Franklin’s opener sets a high bar.
Let It Grow delivers an inspired jam that carries into the second set.
Brent Mydland’s organ shines on Brokedown Palace, adding emotional weight as his final tour.
Loose Lucy and relaxed flow of the second set earn praise in matrix recordings.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is crisp and his solos, especially on "Women Are Smarter" and the jam sections, are inspired; his voice falters early on "Help On The Way" but recovers later.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh delivers a warm, melodic bass with occasional vintage ’73‑style licks that stand out as some of his best of the era.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides solid backing vocals and adds a New Orleans‑flavored second line on the encore; his song calls improve with each listen.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s organ work is energetic, especially on "Brokedown Palace," adding extra drive to the set and carrying emotional weight as one of his final West Coast appearances.
